item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here and is All Rights Reserved. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. Have an upvote.

/DC throws +1 at you

item.title: They Cloned Dr Gerald?!
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, but that was the first thing I thought of when I read this.

Also, throwing him at it could be… interesting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd just like to note that the picture is clearly of Monument Valley but Route 35 goes nowhere near there

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Picture's broke. It was linked off site.

item.title: Re: hmm.
item.content:encoded:
look at the title.

"Playing chicken". That's what they're doing. Over and over and over, for all eternity.

item.title: hmm.
item.content:encoded:
not sure….. so it's a pair of car's that manifest and crash?
I just don't know. -1 till I can figure it out or a little explanation is added.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Containment thoughts:
-Satellite observation is a bit excessive. Some CCTV cameras on poles would do.
-Fencing is cheap and would go a long way towards both keeping out prying eyes and blocking any obstructive debris.
-There should be a bit about clean-up and information suppression if it should hop to another road.
-12 meters is not a minimum safe distance for debris like that.

Description thoughts:
-In the bit about obstructions, "in-deliberately" seems a very odd word choice. Why not "accidentally", "unintentionally", or perhaps "deliberately or not"?
-Should it be mentioned that a fireball is not typical of such a collision? It's not impossible for a high-speed head-on to catch fire but that cinematic fireball seems unlikely enough that it might count as part of the anomalous behavior. Especially in light of the tendency to cause further movie-scale mayhem during "road trips".
-I wonder if ramps count as obstructions. Or thick sand, maybe an oil slick. Something to slow them down or divert the collision without necessarily stopping either car. Presumably testing was canceled before they explored all the possibilities.

I like it. I was prepared not to because ghost cars re-living the fatal crash is too cliche. But you wrote it well and by not attempting a back-story dodged most of the undesirable tropes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a combination Michael Bay movie and math problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It's inexplicable and strange in the right sort of way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. Not badly written and mostly mechanically sound, it just didn't catch my attention. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noted, and going to fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I initially downvoted but was won over by the premise and by the end of the article had changed to an upvote. That said, the article itself feels a little clunky to me. Some suggestions:

+ show block
– hide block

Due to the destruction SCP-1858 may cause,

I would agree that this opening clause is unnecessary and also robs the opening of some of its intrigue.

30-kilometer"/"30 kilometer"/"twelve (12) meters"/"97 km/h"/etc.

Seems to be some inconsistency in how numbers and distances are formatted - suggest sticking to a single convention and standardising (e.g. "30 km"/"12 m"/"97 km/h").

Due to the massive strength of the explosion,

Would again suggest this is unnecessary and seems odd in a clinical report.

Six (6) seconds in the aftermath of SCP-1858's collision,

Not sure if this is a specific phraseology used in car crashes, but 'seconds in the aftermath' just doesn't read correctly for me. I'd suggest 'Six seconds after SCP-1858's collision' myself.

deliberately or in-deliberately

Apparently 'indeliberately' is in fact a word though it doesn't seem to be very frequently used. I'd suggest 'deliberately or otherwise'.

on its original site

'at its original site'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the idea when you (Dr Clockwork) showed it to me some time ago, and I really like how it's presented here. Well done, gentlemen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ahem

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I sort of like how there's no given or implied backstory here. Something about the way it's written makes me want to imagine various explanations for it. Great work. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Directed by Michael Bay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suppose I can try.

Kentucky Fried Criticisms
Thought Provokin' Good

Due to the destruction SCP-1858 may cause

I don't think this is a necessary line, especially considering it doesn't particularly match what comes after it.

to be under remote satellite surveillance at all times

You see? "Because this thing is so destructive, watch it with a satellite" doesn't match very well for me, at least in terms of describing how to contain this thing.

All entrances and exits leading to Route 35 are to be permanently closed off from public access

According to Wikipedia... US Route 35 is 663 kilometers long (412 miles), has 83 major intersections, and passes through three states.
Now, perhaps not all are easily accessed, and perhaps it is not the most traveled road, but to simply say "close off all public access to the entirety of this road" stretches my suspension of disbelief just a bit too far. I may be nitpicking, but I think you could stand to be a little more creative with your containment.

inspected for any barriers and/or debris that can prevent SCP-1858

Could instead of can

SCP-1858 consists of two (2) entities

I would think "vehicles" would be a better word choice than entities, especially considering you call them vehicles every other time.

license plates of both vehicles have been obscured with duct tape

How do we know it's duct tape? Did we get a really clear picture with the satellite or did Researcher McQueen jog out onto the road after it went by and do a visual check?

manifest approximately eight (8) kilometers apart… then accelerate towards each other… speed of approximately 90 km/h

Do they start from a standstill or at speed? How quickly are they accelerating?

collision will be followed by an explosion

What kind? Gas? C4? Nuclear? There's a good variety of explosion types

undetermined strength of the explosion

I'm not an explosionologist, but this seems like the sort of thing the Foundation would be able to figure out, especially if it happens with any amount of regularity

If the usual route of either component

First entities, then vehicles, then SCP-1858-1 and SCP-1858-2, now components. Consistency is a plus.

is deliberately obstructed

What about unintentionally? What constitutes obstruction? Would a branch or something that they need to briefly change lanes to avoid count or does it have to cover the road?

The test logs and/or incident reports

You mention "during scheduled testing" in three logs that are also listed under "incident report." In these tests, the cars appeared somewhere else. What exactly did were they testing? Did they intentionally block roads to see where the cars would go, or did they screw up and not clear the road properly? Was it an incident because there was unexpected test failure, or because there was civilian death, or both, or neither?

A disturbing trend… SCP-1858 appears to have a preference to re-locate onto roads containing heavy traffic and flammable and/or explosive material

It only involved explosive/flammable material in one of four tests, nitpicky, but I don't think you can call that a trend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you sir.

And give Voct some credit as well, he assisted me in developing the title, and for cranking up some new ideas in the experiment logs. (Apologies Voct)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still love this idea enough that even just decent execution is enough for me. Nice work, Clockwork. You've come a long way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you could, may you list them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a bit too rough around the edges for me, but nothing immediately jumps out at me screaming "FIX THIS."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I'll obtain a new file for it in the next hour.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image is busted.

I recommend uploading your file rather than linking it from another site. The latter is considered bad form.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noted, and fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
During scheduled testing on ██/██/20██ during scheduled testing

But upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember this! I offered to do a line by line in chat and forgot. I've actually got a document saved that's for this skip. Well sorry.

Apology upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm, so it's a repeating loop of two cars crashing into each other and blowing up?

Well written. Have an upvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here it goes, I hope this doesn't sink into downvotes. Special thanks to Voct for tweaking the Experiment Log and a few parts of the Description.
